question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not implement any fallback to a secondary database or read replica during a network partition, meaning that a partition affecting only the primary database causes complete unavailability of the topic lookup endpoint even if replicas are still reachable?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the REST-Java service uses a shared HikariCP connection pool for all service calls (TopicService, EntityService, CustomFeeService) during a network partition, meaning that connection exhaustion caused by topicRepository.findById() blocking also prevents entityService.findById() and customFeeService.findById() from acquiring connections, causing a complete failure of the getTopic() endpoint even if only one of the three database tables is unreachable?
